title: httptest, Custom RoundTrippers, Fuzzing, Race Evidence, and Mutations · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers httptest, custom roundtrippers, fuzzing, race evidence, and mutations into a changed risk-layered native Go crawler test system. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of risk-layered native Go crawler test system with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Build original accepted, malformed, adversarial, Unicode, boundary, duplicate, inaccessible, privacy-sensitive, and changed fixtures with provenance.
Use custom RoundTrippers for request, redirect, body, cancellation, cleanup, and deterministic address decisions without network.
Use loopback HTTP and TLS servers for redirects, truncation, compression, encoding, slow headers and bodies, disconnects, retries, and shutdown.

lab case — A lab Go team handles case 3371 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove original fixtures, RoundTrippers, httptest faults, fuzz and race invariants, and mutation detection; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. content.text: A lab Go team handles case 4940 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove original fixtures, RoundTrippers, httptest faults, fuzz and race invariants, and mutation detection; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build original accepted, malformed, adversarial, Unicode, boundary, duplicate, inaccessible, privacy-sensitive, and changed fixtures with provenance.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Mutation Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-mutation-evidence-1j0t18" that uses case 7335 to prove original fixtures, RoundTrippers, httptest faults, fuzz and race invariants, and mutation detection. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Mutation Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Mutation Evidence into observable performance evidence.
